c74object.name: buildspans
c74object.digest: Assembles spans of notes and offsets for a music transcript
c74object.description:
The `buildspans` object is an analysis engine designed to group discrete note events and timing offsets into contiguous "spans" of bars. It operates by maintaining an internal dictionary of "building" spans, organized hierarchically by Palette, Track (which includes an offset identifier), and Bar timestamp.
A span is defined as a contiguous sequence of bars within a single track. The object intelligently determines when to end a span and begin a new one based on two primary mechanisms:
1. **Discontinuity Detection:** - **Temporal Gaps:** A span ends if a newly received timestamp falls more than one bar length beyond the end of the most recent bar in that span. - **Offset Shifts:** If the global offset is updated (Inlet 2) to a value that would create a significant gap relative to the track's existing activity, the current span is finalized.
2. **Intelligent Rating-Based Pruning:** Whenever a new bar begins, the object performs a deferred rating check on the previous bar. It evaluates whether the span is "better" (higher rating) with or without that bar. The rating of a span is calculated as: `(Lowest Mean Score of any Bar in Span) * (Number of Bars in Span)`. A span is finalized and output early if: - Including the most recent bar would decrease the overall span rating. - The most recent bar's individual mean score is significantly higher than the combined rating of the span it would join.
When a span ends, its data is back-propagated to all constituent bars (ensuring they all share the same final rating and span list) and then output through the object's outlets.

inlet.digest: Inlet 1 (Hot): Note Data, Flush, or Clear
inlet.description: The primary control inlet. - **List [Timestamp, Score]:** Adds a note to the current span. If no offset has been set, the first timestamp received here initializes the global offset. - **List [Synth_Timestamp, Score, Orig_Timestamp]:** Adds a note using the first value for bar calculation and the third for storage/output. - **bang:** Triggers a global flush. All currently building spans across all palettes and tracks are finalized and output. Note that track, offset, and palette values **persist** after a bang, while the local bar length is reset. - **clear:** Resets the entire internal state, including all dictionary data and resetting track (0), offset (0.0), loop_start (0.0), and palette ("") to defaults. - **set_bar_buffer [name]:** Sets the buffer~ from which to read the bar length (defaults to "bar").

inlet.digest: Inlet 2 (Cold): Global Offset and Loop Start
inlet.description: Sets the temporal anchor for new spans. - **float:** Sets the global offset in milliseconds. - **list [offset, loop_start]:** Sets both the offset and a loop start point. The loop start is added to relative offsets during absolute bar timestamp calculation. Changing these values may trigger span duplication or discontinuity checks for active tracks. Values persist after a `bang`.
Sets the integer ID for the current track (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This value determines which track incoming notes are assigned to and persists after a `bang`.
Sets the current palette symbol. The object organizes spans separately for each unique palette. This value persists after a `bang`.

Directly sets the bar length (in ms) used for grouping notes. - If set to a positive value, this override is used instead of reading from a buffer. - If set to 0 or less, the object falls back to reading the bar length from the designated `buffer~`. **Crucially, this value is reset to 0 following a `bang` or `clear` message.**

outlet.description: Outputs a list of integers representing the chronological bar timestamps (in ms) that constitute the finalized span. This is typically the primary output used for triggering playback or further analysis.
Outputs the track number (integer) associated with the span that was just output from Outlet 1.

When a span completes, this outlet outputs a sequence of messages for every bar in the span. Each message uses a hierarchical selector in the format: `[track_num]::[bar_timestamp]::[property]`. Available properties include: - **absolutes:** List of high-precision timestamps for all notes in the bar. - **scores:** List of scores corresponding to the notes in `absolutes`. - **mean:** The average score for this specific bar. - **rating:** The final calculated rating for the entire span (shared by all bars). - **offset:** The global offset used when this bar was created. - **span:** The full list of timestamps for the parent span.
Provides verbose status updates, internal process logs (when the `@log` attribute is enabled), and JSON-formatted visualization data (when the `@visualize` attribute is enabled).

method.name: list
method.digest: Add a note to the current span
method.description: Processes a single note event and assigns it to a bar within the active span. - **Format 1: [timestamp, score]** - The timestamp is used for both bar calculation and storage. - **Format 2: [calc_timestamp, score, store_timestamp]** - The first value is used to calculate the bar assignment (allowing for quantizing or shifting), while the third value is the actual timestamp stored and output in the `absolutes` property. If no global offset has been set, the first note received will automatically set the offset to its timestamp.

method.name: bang
method.description: Triggers an immediate finalization of all building spans across all palettes and tracks. Each span is validated, its final rating is calculated, and its data is output through all outlets. **Side Effects:** Resets the local bar length (Inlet 5) to 0, but preserves track number, offset, and palette settings.

method.name: flush
method.description: Immediately ends and outputs all currently building spans for the specified track ID across all palettes.

method.name: clear
method.description: Resets the object to its initial state: - Deletes all internal dictionary data and pending spans. - Resets Track Number to 0. - Resets Global Offset and Loop Start to 0.0. - Resets Palette to an empty symbol. - Resets Local Bar Length to 0.

method.name: set_bar_buffer
method.description: Specifies the name of the `buffer~` object to be used as the source for the bar length timing grid. The object reads the first sample value of this buffer as the bar length in milliseconds. Defaults to "bar".

attribute.name: log
attribute.description: When enabled (1), creates an additional outlet for status information.

attribute.name: visualize
attribute.description: When enabled (1), the object sends JSON data to the connected visualization script.

attribute.name: defer
attribute.description: When enabled (1), state-changing operations and span building logic are moved to the Main (UI) thread using deferral. This ensures that the heavy computational work involved in managing the transcript dictionary does not interfere with the precision of the Scheduler or audio processing.
